free_text: Investigation of 3He-induced reactions on natural Ti
for nuclear analytical and radionuclide production
purposes
Inst. of Nucl. Res. Debrecen, Hungary
Nuclear Research Center, Cairo, Egypt
Abo Akademi Univ. Turku, Finnland
Lawrence Livermore Nat. Lab., Livermore, Ca
Inst. of Physics and Power Engineering,
Obninsk, Russia
China Nucl. Data Cent., China Inst. of
Atomic Energy, Beijing, China
MGC 20E Cyclotron, Abo Akademi Univ.
MGC 20E Cyclotron, Debrecen, Hungary
CV-28 Cyclotron, Juelich,Germany
High purity metallic foils (>99.9%) from Goodfellow.
Al 9 micron
Ti 13.6 micron
Stacked target technique was
used. Stacks contained foils in a Ti-Al seguence. Al
foil served as energy degrader and catcher foil.
Cu foils were used as monitors. Irradiations were done
at 18, 27 and 36 MeV.
Decay data were taken from R.B.Firestone,  V.S.Shirley
[Ed], Table of Isotopes, Wiley Interscience, New York,
Elemental cross section was deterined.
Study of recoiled reaction products were performed.
High purity Ge gamma detector
Several monitor foils
and target foils were placed in the same stack. This
way the whole excitation function of the monitor
reaction was remeasured and compared with the
recommended values. It made possible simultaneous
determination of the bombarding energy and beam
intensity. Also in to check the energy degradation
along the stack.
Chapter 4
Ts
Finalized at NDS
KB Some energy data in Subentry D4112008
were corrected.
No energy uncertainty is given.
The energy attenuation was calculated by
using the polynomial approximation of Andersen and
Ziegler along the stacks.
The average total error of the data was about
Numerical data were taken from Table 2. of the
publication.
Approved by F. Tarkanyi
